PROC-001.title: Annual Review Procedure
PROC-001.description: Step-by-step procedure for conducting the annual policy review cycle in LogicGate, including owner responsibilities, timelines, and approval routing.
PROC-001.text: ﻿Annual Review Process for Compliance Policies and Standards Block, Inc. Last Approved: Oct 30, 2025 Effective Date: Oct 30, 2025 ________________ 1. Overview Introduction and Purpose: The annual review process ensures Block’s policies and standards are reviewed at appropriate intervals to maintain accuracy and effectiveness. Through LogicGate, the Compliance Policy Team manages review dates and notifications, supporting document owners [a]in completing timely review of their documents. This procedure establishes: * How review dates are set in LogicGat; * When notifications are sent to document owners; * Steps for completing the annual review; and * Process for requesting timeline adjustments. The Compliance Policy Team coordinates review timing to align related documents where possible, particularly ensuring standards are reviewed in conjunction with their parent policies for efficiency and consistency. Scope: This procedure applies to all Block Compliance Policies and Standards, including related subsidiary documentation. The process is managed by the Compliance Policy Team and must be followed by all document owners or delegates responsible for these materials. Definitions: Terminology [b] Definition Policy Policies are high level statements of management intent that formally establish requirements to drive compliance and outline the goals, principles, or directives of the Chief Compliance Officer ("CCO"). Policies focus on communicating binding compliance requirements and do not dictate detailed steps. Policies are the overall guidelines for the organization. Standard Standards assist in decision-making and outline further business, technology, risk or other processes, and controls outlined in overarching policies. Standards may lay out the operational strategy and functions that are performed to implement a Policy's requirements. Standards may also establish lifecycle stages, responsibilities, and any additional product, business or market-specific requirements that go beyond what's required in an overarching Policy. Document Owner The Owner is an individual responsible for ensuring that a Document makes its way through the entire Document lifecycle. Responsibilities include: * Identifying the need for potential new Documents and ideating * Creating initial version of Documents * Identifying reviewers and approvers * Planning and conducting awareness campaigns * Conducting scheduled reviews * Understanding the Company's current state of Compliance with a Document * Ensuring forward-looking requirements are accounted for in roadmap planning * Developing and tracking the execution of the implementation plan * Completing attestations to the Document's content prior to publication Annual Review The required process of examining and validating a document[c]'s content at least once every 12 months[d][e] to ensure continued accuracy, relevance, and effectiveness. Review Date The date by which a document must complete its annual review cycle, typically set as the anniversary of the last review unless adjusted to align with related documents or committee schedules. Extension Request A formal request to extend a document's review date beyond its scheduled timeline. Extensions are limited to a maximum of 6 months, require C-Core + 1 approval, and must include business justification. Only one extension is permitted per review cycle.[f] Material Changes Substantive changes to a Document's requirements or guidance impacting the goals, principles, processes, or directives. Includes new requirements, more restrictive requirements, expansion of scope, or changes that materially impact business operations. Immaterial Changes Non-substantive changes to a Document's content that do not impact requirements or guidance. May include updating grammar, formatting, correcting naming conventions, or updating links. LogicGate Block's system of record for managing compliance policies, standards, and procedures, including the annual review process and related workflows. ________________ 2. Annual Review Procedure Requirements 1. Review Date Management The Compliance Policy Team manages all review dates within LogicGate and has primary responsibility for coordinating the annual review process. When setting review dates, the Policy Team will align related documents to ensure efficient review cycles, particularly striving to align standards alongside their parent policies whenever possible. The Compliance Policy Team will: * Set initial review dates based on the last approval date ; * Adjust dates to align with committee schedules; [g] * Coordinate related document reviews; * Monitor completion status; and * Maintain accurate tracking. 2.2 Notification Process LogicGate automatically generates notifications to Document Owners at three key points in the review cycle: At 90 calendar days before the review due date, Document Owners receive their initial notification via email. Upon receipt, Document Owners or delegates should begin planning their review process, including identifying necessary stakeholders and scheduling review meetings. At 60 calendar days before the due date, a second notification via email prompts Document Owners to ensure their review is actively in progress. By this point, Document Owners should be gathering stakeholder input and documenting any changes needed. The final notification arrives via email 30 calendar days before the due date. This notification serves as both a completion reminder and the deadline for submitting any extension requests. [h] 2.3 Document Review Requirements Document Owners must complete their reviews in accordance with the lifecycle requirements established in the Block, Inc. Compliance Policy on Policies (“PoP”). The full review and approval process, including stakeholder engagement, quality control and approval requirements are detailed in the PoP. For the complete document review process, including creation, revision, approval, and publication requirements, Document Owners should refer to the PoP. [i][j] 2.4 Extension Process Document Owners requiring additional time beyond the scheduled review date must submit a formal extension request through LogicGate. All extension requests must be submitted before the current review date and include a detailed business justification. [k] Extension approval authority is structured as follows: * Extensions require C-Core+1 member approval[l] * No extension may extend past six months from the original scheduled due date * Only one extension is permitted per annual review cycle Upon receiving an extension request, the Compliance Policy Team will review for completeness and route to the appropriate approver. Once approved, the Compliance Policy Team will update LogicGate with the new review dates and adjust the notification schedule accordingly.[m] The Compliance Policy Team will maintain documentation of all extension requests and approvals. [n][o]Documents will be reported as extended on reporting status reports. 2.5 Board and Committee Review Alignment The Compliance Policy Team regularly reviews committee schedules and may adjust document review dates to align with the next available Board or Committee meeting. This ensures efficient review cycles and appropriate governance oversight. When adjusting review dates for Committee or Board alignment, the Compliance Policy Team will: * Identify the next available Committee or Board date, as applicable; * Update the document review date in LogicGate; * Notify Document Owners of the revised timeline;and * Adjust automated notifications accordingly. Documents whose review dates are adjusted to align with the next available Committee or Board meeting will not be reported as overdue in compliance reporting. This exception applies only to documents actively scheduled for Committee or Board review and does not apply to other delays or extensions. Document Owners should note that the Compliance Policy Team may adjust their document’s review date to either align with related documents (such as Standards with their parent policies) or to accommodate Committee or Board scheduling. These adjustments will be communicated to Document Owners and reflected in LogicGate notifications. [p] 2.6 Documentation and Evidence All review activities must be documented within LogicGate to maintain a clear audit trail. The Compliance Policy Team maintains responsibility for ensuring proper documentation is retained, while Document Owners are responsible for providing evidence. [q] 2.6.1 Current Documentation (2025 Forward) LogicGate serves as the system of record for all review activities starting in November 2025. Required documentation includes: * Review completion records; * Extension requests and approvals; * Date adjustment justification; and * Committee alignment documentation. 2.6.2 Historical Documentation (Pre-November 2025) For documentation of reviews conducted prior to November 2025, Document Owners should contact the Compliance Policy Team directly. The Compliance Policy Team maintains historical records and can provide evidence of past reviews, approvals, and related documentation upon request. The Compliance Policy Team will maintain all available records according to retention requirements specified in the PoP. All documentation should remain accessible for audit purposes and regulatory examinations. [r] 2.7 Reporting and Escalation 2.7.1 Status Reporting The Compliance Policy Team monitors review completion status and provides regulator [s]reporting through multiple channels: * Monthly Reporting: * Review status dashboard with Compliance Policy Governance leadership * Overdue document reporting * Upcoming review schedule for next 90 calendar calendar days * Extension tracking and status [t] * Quarterly Report to CPC: * Comprehensive review status[u] * Overdue documents requiring attention * Extension trends and patterns * Significant timeline adjustments [v] * Committee schedule impacts 2.7.2 Overdue Documents: Documents are considered overdue when: * Review is not completed by the due date * No extension requests has been submitted * Extension request was not approved * Extension period has expired Note: Documents pending next available Committee or Board review are not reported as overdue, provided they are actively scheduled for Committee or Board review. 2.7.3 Escalation Process: For overdue documents, the Compliance Policy Team will: * Send immediate notification to the owner via LogicGate * Include in monthly reporting to Compliance Leadership * Report to CPC in quarterly updates [w] * Track remediation efforts Document owners of overdue documents must: * Provide a timely status update * Submit extension request if needed * Develop remediation timeline * Provide progress updates until resolved The Compliance Policy Team maintains oversight of overdue documents until review completion is confirmed in LogicGate. ________________ 3. Compliance & Enforcement 1. Roles and Responsibilities: Block, Inc. Roles: * Compliance Policy Team * Manages LogicGate review dates and notifications * Maintains documentation and reporting * Coordinates Committee and Board scheduling * Escalates overdue reviews * Reports to Compliance Policy Committee and Compliance Leadership * Provides historical documentation upon request * Document Owners[x] * Completes reviews by due dates * Follows PoP document lifecycle process * Submits extension requests when needed * Prepares committee materials * Maintains document accuracy * Confirms review completion in LogicGate * C-Core + 1 Members: * Reviews/Approves extensions * Supports overdue document escalation * Compliance Leadership: * Reviews Monthly status reporting * Monitors overdue documents * Ensures program effectiveness[y] * Compliance Policy Committee (“CPC”) * Reviews quarterly status reports during meetings, as needed * Provides governance oversight * Addresses systemic issues 2. Monitoring and Reporting On an ongoing basis, the Procedure Owner [z]and/or their designee monitors activities for compliance and objective achievement. As outlined in the Block, Inc. Compliance Management System Policy, Compliance will provide regular updates, as applicable, to the Block, Inc. Board and executive management. Reporting may cover specific information related to: * Policies and standards * Procedures * Audit/exam findings * Issues management * Training * Complaints[aa] 1. Record Retention All Documents are retained according to the longer of: * Block’s Data Classification & Handling Policy * Applicable regulatory requirements 1. Exceptions The Procedure Owner or designee will have authority to approve exceptions and/or exemptions to this Procedure, provided that the proposed exception or exemption does not violate applicable laws and regulations. Exception requests must be documented in writing,[ab] include a reason for a request, and provide a description of the controls that the Company will implement to mitigate risks associated with the exception. The Procedure Owner is responsible for retaining evidence of exception requests and related approvals. ________________ 4. Questions & Contact Information Questions: For questions or clarifications about this Procedure, please contact the Procedure Owner. Review related documents: * Compliance Policy on Policies ________________ 5. Document Details & Revisions Review and Approval: This document shall be reviewed and approved at least triennially and on an ad hoc basis in accordance with the Compliance Document Approval Matrix. Approval History: Approver Title Type Description Date Stevi Winer Compliance Policy & Mandatory Training Lead Initial Approval Initial creation and approval of procedure 10/29/2025 Document Details: Business Block Scope Global Compliance Domain Compliance Governance Document Owner Compliance Policy & Mandatory Training Manager Approved By Compliance Policy & Mandatory Training Lead [a]If this is a named role within the doc, I'd like us to consistently capitalize this in our docs [b]How do we determine the order of terms? It's not alphabetical and would be easier to scan if alphabetical [c]In some instances we capitalized and in others we don't. Need to be consistent and always check for this in other docs. Preference for me is capitalization throughout. [d]Is it a year for everything? That could be inferred from this [e]Recall we had different timing requirements previously by tier so just pressure testing [f]How do we enforce / control for this? [g]Just confirming do we have an upper limit on this in the PoP? For instance, adjust dates within a 90 day period max. [h]Is this correct? [i]Please note we will be building out a procedure on this in Q4 and at that time will replace the references to the procedure [j]What is the status of this? Presuming once that is stood up / created, this will no longer refer to the PoP? Is that a correct interpretation? [k]Earlier we say 30 days before is the due date, just confirming if that is correct or if it can be any time before the due date? Whatever it is should be written the same way in both places. [l]How does this compare to our approval matrix? Aren't most approvers C-core + 1 [m]Is this manual? [n]Please note we will be building out a procedure on this in Q4 and at that time will replace the references to the procedure [o]What is the status of this? Presuming once that is stood up / created, this will no longer refer to the PoP? Is that a correct interpretation? [p]For my understanding, how does this occur? [q]Did this sentence get cut off? Providing evidence of what? If it is the approved policy, that typically sits with us but if it is evidence of adherence to the policy then yes that would sit outside of the policy team. I might even suggest we only list it as out of scope and not specify it is the Doc Owner since I envision there are some instances where it my be the product team. [r]Do we need this in here? [s]regular? [t]Where is this shared? Asking as this will be relevant for GO-01 [u]If this is just an overall view of our doc inventory can we say that? Review suggest we are cataloging based on where the docs are at in their lifecycle and I don't think we mean that [v]How do we define significant? [w]We need to ensure we include this as it may come up on POL-01 validation [x]Should we add Doc Approvers? [y]Do we need this? [z]Who will this be? [aa]Does this make sense to include? [ab]Where?

PROC-002.title: Compliance Document Management Procedure
PROC-002.description: How to create, version, and manage compliance documents within LogicGate, including document lifecycle stages and required metadata.
PROC-002.text: ﻿Compliance Document Management Procedure Block, Inc. Last Approved: Dec 9, 2025 Effective Date: Jan 5, 2026 ________________ 1. Overview Introduction and Purpose: This Compliance Document Management Procedure (the "Procedure") outlines Block's approach to managing compliance Documents through the full Document lifecycle in the Document management system. This Procedure supports the framework in the Block, Inc. Compliance Policy on Policies (“PoP”) to ensure documentation is accurate and supports current laws, regulations, and operational practices. Scope: This Procedure applies to all Block Compliance Policies, Standards, and Procedures including related subsidiary Documents. The process is managed by the Compliance Policy Team and must be followed by all Document Owners or delegates responsible for these materials. Definitions: Terminology Definition Document A Policy, Standard, or Procedure. Policy Policies are high level statements of management intent that formally establish requirements to drive compliance and outline the goals, principles, or directives of the Chief Compliance Officer (“CCO”). Policies focus on communicating binding compliance requirements and do not dictate detailed steps. Policies are the overall guidelines for the organization. Standard Standards assist in decision-making and outline further business, technology, risk or other processes, and controls outlined in overarching policies. Standards may lay out the operational strategy and functions that are performed to implement a Policy’s requirements. Standards may also establish lifecycle stages, responsibilities, and any additional product, business or market-specific requirements that go beyond what’s required in an overarching Policy. Procedure Procedures change frequently and describe steps that implement a Policy or process to comply with a regulation or Standard. Procedures focus on a process and desired outcome between multiple teams. Procedures exist to standardize a process and provide guidance on timing and output. Request ID Number assigned through the Document management system for each new request. Compliance Document Approval Matrix Framework that specifies which roles or governance bodies must sign off on Document changes based on the hierarchy of the Document and the scale of the change. Material Changes Substantive changes to a Document's requirements or guidance impacting the goals, principles, processes, or directives. Includes new requirements, more restrictive requirements, expansion of scope, or changes that materially impact business operations. Immaterial Changes Non-substantive changes to a Document's content that do not impact requirements or guidance. May include updating grammar, formatting, correcting naming conventions, or updating links. LogicGate Block's Document management system for managing compliance policies, standards, and procedures, including the annual review process and related workflows. Tier 1 Document * Limited to Policies only (Standards / Procedures are excluded) * Policies with a regulatory requirement to be approved by the applicable Block or US Board * Policies that outline enterprise-wide framework and/or overarching requirements that apply across multiple significant brands, entities or businesses * Policies that will have material business impact across multiple brands, entities or businesses * Policies that present new or expanded responsibilities for the applicable Block Board or a US Subsidiary Board * Policies that are more like high level frameworks that are unlikely to change significantly over the year Tier 2 Document * All other Policies that do not meet Tier 1 criteria * Policies and Standards that are core to key Compliance laws, rules, and regulations * Policies and Standards that outlines more technical and brand specific guidance Tier 3 Document * All Standards that do not meet Tier 1 or Tier 2 criteria * Standards that support key Compliance laws, rules, and regulations * Standards that outline day-to-day management and operational activities to execute against existing Policies or Standards (approved at Tier 1 or 2 levels) ________________ 2. Procedure Requirements 1. Document Creation & Update Management 2.1.1 Request Submission: The Submitter will access the Document management system to submit a new Document request or request change to an existing Document. The request will contain the Document type, business need, scope, urgency, and supplemental documentation as applicable (e.g. regulatory guidance, audit report, etc.). The Document management system will automatically assign a Request ID and notify the Compliance Policy Team. If the Submitter is not the Document Owner, the Document Owner will also review the request and have the authority to add additional stakeholders to review the request. 2.1.2 Conflict Checks with Existing Documents: The Compliance Policy Team will review the existing Document library to identify potential overlaps or conflicts with current Policies, Standards or Procedures. The Compliance Policy Team will Document findings in the Document management system and consult with Document owners of related Documents as needed. 2.1.3 Document Request Form Handling: The Compliance Policy Team will conduct an initial assessment of the request to ensure completeness and request additional information from the Submitter as needed. The Compliance Policy Team will also assess the Document hierarchy to determine if the intended purpose and level of detail for the Policy, Standard or Procedure aligns with the hierarchy in Compliance Policy on Policies | Block. Priority assignment may be based on business needs and regulatory requirements. 2.1.4 Template Provision and Guidance: Documents must be drafted on appropriate templates provided to the Submitter by the Compliance Policy Team based on Document type, and must follow current brand guidelines regarding elements including but not limited to font and formatting with approvals Documented in an approval history table. The Compliance Policy Team will consult with the Submitter as needed to clarify requirements and communicate timeline expectations. 2.2 Quality Control Review The Compliance Policy Team will review the information provided by the Submitter and assess the feasibility of the request, considering factors such as the urgency of the request, requested completion date, resource allocation, competing priorities and determination of approval routing. After the feasibility has been assessed, the Compliance Policy Team will communicate the estimated completion timeline and approve the request or send the request back to the Submitter for additional information. 2.2.1 Initial Quality Control The Compliance Policy Team will conduct a Quality Control (“QC”) review of the Document in accordance with the Compliance Policy on Policies prior to routing for stakeholder review or document approval. The QC review includes, but is not limited to: * Verifying all sections are complete and formatted in accordance with the template * Checking for consistency in terminology and definitions * Validating accuracy of cross-references to other documents * Confirming clarity, grammar and readability 2.2.2 Post Approval Quality Control [a][b][c] Prior to publication, the Compliance Policy Team will conduct a final QC review to ensure accuracy with the approver name(s), approval date(s) and formatting. 2.3 Retirement 2.3.1 Retirement Request Initiation: Documents may warrant retirement for reasons including but not limited to: * Existing Document superseded by new Document * Guidance no longer applicable * Document consolidated with another Document * Regulatory change To initiate Document retirement, the Document Owner or the Compliance Policy Team must first identify the Document for retirement. Next, the Document Owner or the Compliance Policy Team will submit the retirement request through the Document management system workflow and Document the reason for retirement, effective date of retirement and replacement Document when applicable. The Document Owner is responsible for conducting an impact assessment to identify affected stakeholders and processes and updating the Document management system accordingly. 2.3.2 Stakeholder Review: The Document management system will notify Stakeholder Reviewers of the Document proposed for retirement. If a replacement Document exists, Stakeholder Reviewers will also review the replacement Document for accuracy. Stakeholder Reviewers have a review period of 15 business days to provide feedback or object to retirement with justification. The Compliance Policy Team evaluates feedback and objections to determine the appropriate course of action. 2.3.3 Approval Workflow: Approvals for retiring Documents follows the same approval process as the original Document as outlined in the Compliance Policy on Policies | Block. Approvers will confirm the appropriateness of the retirement and ensure the impact is fully understood. Approvers will review other Documents to identify references to the retired Document that must be updated. 2.3.4 Retirement Execution: The Compliance Policy Team is responsible for updating the status of the Document to “Retired” in the Document management system and updating the effective date of retirement. The date of retirement and the final approval will be recorded in the Document management system with supplemental documentation (e.g. meeting minutes). The Compliance Policy Team will also remove the retired Document from the published library, and update the title and / or watermark of the retired Document to indicate its retirement status. The retirement notice will also be added to the Document, indicating the retirement date and reason. If a replacement Document exists, the Compliance Policy Team will add a referral to the new Document. 2.3.5 Communication and Record Keeping: The Compliance Policy Team will send a retirement notice to all previous Document recipients to inform of the retirement reason, effective date of retirement and replacement Document (if applicable). The Compliance Policy Team will update all related Documents to remove references to the retired Document in active Documents, and document the change in the revision history. Retired Documents must be retained in accordance with the Compliance Policy on Policies| Block and the Data Classification and Handling Policy | Block. 2.4 Publication and Storage Drafting and editing of compliance documents take place in a Google Doc that is accessed through the Document management system. This Google Doc serves as the authoritative working version throughout the drafting and revision process. Document Owners, Reviewers and other contributors use suggestion mode to ensure revisions are clearly redlined as the document progresses through review and approval. Once all required approvals are completed and documented in the SOR, the Compliance Policy Team finalizes the master Google Doc by incorporating approved changes. A PDF version is then generated from the finalized Google Doc and the link to this PDF is published in the SOR within the appropriate policy, standard, or procedure library on the employee dashboard. All full time employees have access to the published PDF unless specific access restrictions are requested. In such cases, the Document Owner is responsible for notifying the Compliance Policy Team so that access limitations can be applied. 2.4.1 Version Management Document versions are maintained in the SOR, which identifies and displays the active version for all employees. Documents are versioned using approval dates, and the most recent approval date determines the current official version. This approval date is also included in the Document title to ensure clarity and consistency across all published Documents. The SOR preserves a complete version history and clearly marks prior versions as retired once a new version is approved and published. Retired versions are removed from general employee access but remain available to Document Owners and authorized users. 2.4.2 Storage It contains the master Google Doc for each Document, as well as the finalized PDF versions that are published to the SOR. The repository also retains PDF copies of the redline Google Docs that are generated before changes are resolved in the master copy and finalized for publication. These redline PDFs support audit requests and provide a complete historical record of revisions. Retired and archived versions are stored in accordance with established retention rules. Access to this repository is restricted to Compliance Policy Team members and designated leadership. Only the Compliance Policy Team is authorized to modify folder structures, manage permissions, or reorganize records. Special access may be granted when required for authorized purposes, including but not limited to audits and examination teams. 2.4.3. Archival When a Document version is replaced or retired, the previous version is moved to the designated Archive folder within the Compliance Google Drive. Archived Documents must be clearly labeled as archived in their titles to ensure accurate identification and must be retained in accordance with all applicable regulatory and internal retention requirements. Archived materials are maintained in a read only state and cannot be modified. They may be shared with authorized users upon request. Document Owners retain ongoing access through the SOR to the archived versions of the Documents they own. Audit and Examination teams are granted controlled permissions to view archived Documents in both the centralized repository and the SOR. Once a Document is retired, it is archived in the Compliance Google Drive and removed from general employee access within the SOR to ensure that only the current, approved version is available for operational use. 2.5 Communication & Awareness 2.5.1 General Staff Updates General Staff updates are intended to provide the entire company, compliance organization, or functional teams with timely updates about the publication of new and updated Documents. * The Compliance Policy and Mandatory Training Team (“Compliance Policy Team”) will send a monthly general notification to announce the publication of new and updated Documents via Slack to the compliance organization. * The Compliance Policy Team will send more targeted Slack notifications to affected individuals or teams for functional policies. * Both the monthly general notification and the targeted notification will include: Document name, effective date, summary of changes, and a link to the published Document in the Document management system. 2.5.2 Compliance Leadership Reporting * Reporting to Compliance Leadership will include: * New documents, * Documents undergoing revision, * Documents that were retired during the reporting period, * Document review status for documents in a current draft workflow, and * exception metrics based on exceptions submitted through LogicGate. * The Compliance Policy Team will compile a monthly dashboard on document status which is sent to the Chief Compliance Officer. * The Compliance Policy Team will provide an update on policies and metrics to the CPC at its scheduled meetings, but at least quarterly. * The Compliance Policy Team will prepare an annual report to the Block Board of Directors highlighting compliance Document program health with supporting metrics. * For policy updates that are urgent or are a result of significant changes to laws or regulations, the Compliance Policy Team will report on an ad-hoc basis and to the appropriate audience. * On an as-needed basis, the Compliance Policy Team will prepare executive summaries for leadership. 2.5.3 Stakeholder Notifications Document Owners are responsible for driving awareness campaigns for newly published or materially changed Documents to key stakeholders. Awareness campaigns generally include announcements, email notification, or trainings. If the Document Owner deems a policy update as critical[d], the Owner or Delegate should immediately notify identified stakeholders. The notification should generally include what changed, how to comply, and any details on an impact analysis. ________________ 3. Compliance & Enforcement 1. Roles and Responsibilities: Block, Inc. Roles: * Compliance Policy Team * Manages LogicGate review dates and notifications * Maintains documentation and reporting * Coordinates Committee and Board scheduling * Escalates overdue reviews * Reports to Compliance Policy Committee and Compliance Leadership * Provides historical documentation upon request * Submitter: * The individual who submits a Document in the Document management system. * Document Owners * Completes reviews by due dates * Follows PoP document lifecycle process * Submits extension requests when needed * Prepares committee materials * Maintains document accuracy * Confirms review completion in LogicGate * C-Core + 1 Members: * Reviews/Approves extensions * Supports overdue document escalation * Compliance Leadership: * Reviews Monthly status reporting * Monitors overdue documents * Ensures program effectiveness * Compliance Policy Committee (“CPC”) * Reviews quarterly status reports during meetings, as needed * Provides governance oversight * Addresses systemic issues 2. Monitoring and Reporting On an ongoing basis, the Procedure Owner and/or their designee monitors activities for compliance and objective achievement. As outlined in the Compliance Management System Policy, Compliance will provide regular updates, as applicable, to the Block, Inc. Board and executive management. Reporting may cover specific information related to: * Policies and Standards * Procedures * Audit/exam findings * Issues management * Training * Complaints 1. Record Retention All Documents are retained according to the longer of: * Block’s Data Classification & Handling Policy * Applicable regulatory requirements 1. Exceptions The Procedure Owner or designee will have authority to approve exceptions and/or exemptions to this Procedure, provided that the proposed exception or exemption does not violate applicable laws and regulations. Exception requests must be documented in writing, include a reason for a request, and provide a description of the controls that the Company will implement to mitigate risks associated with the exception. The Procedure Owner is responsible for retaining evidence of exception requests and related approvals. 2. Confidentiality This Document is confidential and should only be accessed by employees. ________________ 4. Questions & Contact Information Questions: For questions or clarifications about this Procedure, please contact the Procedure Owner. Review related documents: * Compliance Policy on Policies | Block ________________ 5. Document Details & Revisions Review and Approval: This Document shall be reviewed and approved at least triennially and on an ad hoc basis in accordance with the Compliance Document Approval Matrix. Approval History: Approver Title Type Description Date [Type @ followed by the person’s name] Select [Month date, YYYY] [Type @ followed by the person’s name] Select [Month date, YYYY] Document Details: Business Block Scope Global Compliance Domain Compliance Governance Document Owner Kate Ellingsen (She / Her) Approved By Stevi Winer [a]adding @steviwiner@block.xyz comment "Should we add some language in here about requirements for teams to submit documents for QC and proper lead time (noting this is a requirement for publication)?" [b]@kellingsen@squareup.com @pbuchmann@block.xyz do we want to establish a minimum lead time requirement for submissions to be handled by? proposed language in 2.1.1 above [c]My hesitation is that the lead time is a Nice to Have, and we may paint ourselves into a corner. [d](from the POP) question for team/Stevi - we define materiality so do we need to or want to allow for another subjective assessment of updates being "critical" or not to the Document Owner? if not, we can strike this sentence

PROC-003.title: Compliance Policy Governance Approval Processes Procedure
PROC-003.description: Governance approval workflows in LogicGate — how policies move through board, committee, and owner approval stages.
PROC-003.text: ﻿Compliance Policy Governance Approval Processes Procedure Block, Inc. Last Approved: Apr 28, 2025 Effective Date: Jan 1, 2026 ________________ 1. Overview Introduction and Purpose: This Policy Governance Approval Processes Procedure ("Procedure") outlines Block's approach to managing approval workflows for compliance documents through appropriate governance bodies including committees and the Board of Directors. Scope: This Procedure applies to Block, Inc., its subsidiaries, and affiliates (collectively, “Block”), including all products. Definitions: Terminology Definition Document A Policy, Standard, or Procedure. Policy Policies are high level statements of management intent that formally establish requirements to drive compliance and outline the goals, principles, or directives of the Chief Compliance Officer (“CCO”). Policies focus on communicating binding compliance requirements and do not dictate detailed steps. Policies are the overall guidelines for the organization. Standard Standards assist in decision-making and outline further business, technology, risk or other processes, and controls outlined in overarching policies. Standards may lay out the operational strategy and functions that are performed to implement a Policy’s requirements. Standards may also establish lifecycle stages, responsibilities, and any additional product, business or market-specific requirements that go beyond what’s required in an overarching Policy. Procedure Procedures change frequently and describe steps that implement a Policy or process to comply with a regulation or Standard. Procedures focus on a process and desired outcome between multiple teams. Procedures exist to standardize a process and provide guidance on timing and output. Tier 1 Document * Limited to Policies only (Standards / Procedures are excluded) * Policies with a regulatory requirement to be approved by the applicable Block or US Board * Policies that outline enterprise-wide framework and/or overarching requirements that apply across multiple significant brands, entities or businesses * Policies that will have material business impact across multiple brands, entities or businesses * Policies that present new or expanded responsibilities for the applicable Block Board or a US Subsidiary Board * Policies that are more like high level frameworks that are unlikely to change significantly over the year Tier 2 Document * All other Policies that do not meet Tier 1 criteria * Policies and Standards that are core to key Compliance laws, rules, and regulations * Policies and Standards that outlines more technical and brand specific guidance Tier 3 Document * All Standards that do not meet Tier 1 or Tier 2 criteria * Standards that support key Compliance laws, rules, and regulations * Standards that outline day-to-day management and operational activities to execute against existing Policies or Standards (approved at Tier 1 or 2 levels) ________________ 2. Procedure Requirements 2.1 Governance Approval Process 2.1.1 CPC Approved Documents The Compliance Policy and Mandatory Training Team (“Policy Team”) is responsible for identifying documents requiring Compliance Policy Committee (“CPC”) approval at the next available meeting. The Governance Approval Matrix in the Compliance Policy on Policies is used to ensure the proper documents are presented to CPC and applicable Boards. The Policy Team is responsible for maintaining the Governance Approval Matrix. The Policy Team prepares the CPC agenda using the CPC agenda template that can be found in the Team’s shared drive. The information includes: * Documents being presented for approval and the link to the redline version * Documents being retired * Other matters that may require a CPC vote or for information purposes such as Tier 2 policies approved by international boards in the past quarter The agenda should be drafted at least two weeks in advance of the scheduled meeting date and sent to Leadership for review and comment. The agenda should be finalized a week before the scheduled meeting and sent to CPC members for a pre-read via slack or email communications. * CPC members should be added to permissions for each document as a commenter and the document owner should be an editor to address any comments during the read ahead period and the meeting. At the meeting, the Policy Team is responsible for announcing the agenda, introducing each document and topic, asking if there are questions, soliciting a motion and a second, and determining whether a document is approved, approved with conditions, or whether a document does not receive the requisite approval. * The Policy Team is responsible for taking notes to later create minutes with details related to each document presented. After the meeting the Policy Team is responsible for managing and closing any conditions related to any document and publishing the documents to the SOR. * Following publication the Policy Team is responsible for sending out the CPC notes using the template. * Evidence of approval should be added to the appropriate LogicGate document record during the publication process. Tier 1 documents that are up for Board approval go through the same CPC process as Tier 2 documents except the documents are presented to CPC for recommendation to the Board for approval. The agenda is divided between the two tiers of documents if both are being considered during the meeting and the result of the CPC decision on Tier 1 documents is communicated to the Corporate Governance team to manage the documents through the Board process. 2.1.2 Board Approved Documents For Tier 1 documents the Corporate Governance team creates a form for the Board meeting with the expected documents to be presented. The Policy Team checks to make sure the document count is accurate and the correct documents are represented. The Board form also contains specific material due dates that must be met. The Board documents may already be under review by the Corporate Governance team before the CPC meeting. The Policy Team works with Document Owners and delegates to review and prepare documents for CPC and board consideration and manage timelines to ensure the documents are ready to submit either to Corporate Governance on their timeline or for the read ahead materials to be sent to CPC members. * The Policy Team’s review includes proper template and required language, formatting, and provides comments and minor updates for the document Document Owner to consider prior to submission. 2.1.3 Tracking Pending Updates and Approvals The Document management system automatically sends document owners and submitters notifications 90, 60, and 30 days before the document is due. * The Policy Team monitors the dashboard in LogicGate for documents within the 90 day update window and assigns documents to Policy Team members to begin working with document owners on updates. * Once assigned, the respective Policy Team member will see the document through to committee and board submission or to approval and publication for Tier 3 documents. * Tier 1 and 2 documents must be approved in the month they were last approved if practicable based on meeting schedules. If the meeting schedule does not align then the approval must be at the next available meeting. For Tier 3 documents, approvals should be as close to the date last approved, but no longer than the last approval month unless and approved extension is logged in LogicGate. * For documents at risk of missing approval dates, the team will escalate this information to the Document Owner’s leader within a reasonable time to allow for the deadline to be met. For documents that have missed the approval deadline and that do not have an approved extension, the document will be added to the quarterly report as past due and an action plan must be in place for updating the document as soon as practicable. * The Policy Team will provide quarterly reporting to leadership on governance approval metrics and will provide the CPC information about the volume of documents pending approval at the next meeting. ________________ 3. Compliance & Enforcement 1. Roles and Responsibilities: Block, Inc. Roles: * Role 1: Policy Team * The Policy Team is responsible for managing the governance approval process which includes: * Monitoring and preparing documents for submission to the CPC and any boards for approval * Managing the CPC agenda * Managing the CPC meeting and taking detailed minutes regarding voting, outcome of votes, and any action items * * Role 2: Document Owner * Description * Role 2: Corporate Governance 1. Monitoring and Reporting On an ongoing basis, the Procedure Owner and/or their designee monitors activities for compliance and objective achievement. As outlined in the Compliance Management System Policy, Compliance will provide regular updates, as applicable, to the Block, Inc. Board and executive management. Reporting may cover specific information related to: * Policies and standards * Procedures * Audit/exam findings * Issues management * Training * Complaints 1. Record Retention All Documents are retained according to the longer of: * Block’s Data Classification & Handling Policy * Applicable regulatory requirements 1. Exceptions The Procedure Owner or designee will have authority to approve exceptions and/or exemptions to this Procedure, provided that the proposed exception or exemption does not violate applicable laws and regulations. Exception requests must be documented in writing, include a reason for a request, and provide a description of the controls that the Company will implement to mitigate risks associated with the exception. The Procedure Owner is responsible for retaining evidence of exception requests and related approvals. 2. Confidentiality This document is confidential and should only be accessed by employees. ________________ 4. Questions & Contact Information Questions: For questions or clarifications about this Procedure, please contact the Procedure Owner. Review related documents: * Document Name * Document Name * Document Name ________________ 5. Document Details & Revisions Review and Approval: This document shall be reviewed and approved at least triennially and on an ad hoc basis in accordance with the Compliance Document Approval Matrix. Approval History: Approver Title Type Description Date [Type @ followed by the person’s name] Select [Month date, YYYY] [Type @ followed by the person’s name] Select [Month date, YYYY] Document Details: Business Block Scope [Global/Regional and/or Entity/Region] Compliance Domain Compliance Governance Document Owner [Title] Approved By [Title]

PROC-004.title: Compliance Policy Exception Management Procedure
PROC-004.description: How to submit, track, and resolve policy exceptions and due date extensions in LogicGate.
PROC-004.text: ﻿Compliance Policy Exception Management Procedure [Block, Inc.] Last Approved: Jan 16, 2026 Effective Date: Jan 16, 2026 ________________ 1. Overview Introduction and Purpose: This Exception Management Procedure (the "Procedure") outlines Block's approach to receiving, evaluating, approving, and tracking Exceptions to compliance policies and Procedures through the SOR workflow system. Scope:This Procedure applies to Block, Inc., its subsidiaries, and affiliates (collectively, “Block”), including all products. Definitions: Terminology Definition Document A Policy, Standard or Procedure. Exception Any deviations from requirements or processes dictated in a given Document. Policy Policies are high level statements of management intent that formally establish requirements to drive compliance and outline the goals, principles, or directives of the Chief Compliance Officer (“CCO”). Policies focus on communicating binding compliance requirements and do not dictate detailed steps. Policies are the overall guidelines for the organization. Procedure Procedures change frequently and describe steps that implement a Policy or process to comply with a regulation or Standard. Procedures focus on a process and desired outcome between multiple teams. Procedures exist to Standardize a process and provide guidance on timing and output. Standard Standards assist in decision-making and outline further business, technology, risk or other processes, and controls outlined in overarching policies. Standards may lay out the operational strategy and functions that are performed to implement a Policy’s requirements. Standards may also establish lifecycle stages, responsibilities, and any additional product, business or market-specific requirements that go beyond what’s required in an overarching Policy. System of Record (SOR) System of Record (SOR) is the designated system used to manage the approval workflows, governance controls, lifecycle management, and authorized access for approved compliance Documents. ________________ 2. Procedure Requirements 2.1. Request Submission Exception requests may be initiated by any employee who requires temporary relief from an established Policy, Standard, or Procedure. Exceptions may apply to policies, underlying Standards, and Procedures, and a single request may reference multiple Documents. Requests must be submitted through the System of Record (SOR), where the requestor provides all required information, identifies the Exception Owner[a], specifies the requested validity period, and attaches or links any supporting materials. Exception validity periods may not exceed six months and must be defined at the time of submission. Optional stakeholder reviewers may be included when needed. The Exception Owner is determined based on the highest tier of the Document included in the Exception request. [placeholder][b] Once submitted, the request is routed to the Central Team for governance review. Multiple Exception requests may exist for the same Document, and more than one may remain active at the same time. 2.2. Review and Approvals The Central Team conducts the initial review to verify completeness and determine whether the request meets the criteria for Exception consideration. After validation, the request proceeds sequentially through designated stakeholder reviewers. A rejection at any point terminates the workflow, and a new request must be submitted if reconsideration is desired. After stakeholder review, the request advances to the Exception Owner. The Exception Owner is responsible for reviewing the business justification for the Exception, and evaluating the impact assessment and mitigation plans provided by the requestor. The Exception Owner must determine whether the proposed mitigation and eventual closure plans are sufficient to manage the associated risks. Approval should only be granted when the Exception Owner believes these measures adequately address the identified impacts. The Exception Owner may also adjust the validity period if warranted and then renders a decision. 2.3. Exception Validity and Renewal Exceptions remain active for the validity period defined in the approved request. The SOR issues automated reminders 30 days prior to expiration to ensure timely action. The requestor must either renew or close the Exception before it expires. Renewals require submission of a new request and are subject to the same review and approval process to validate ongoing justification and governance oversight. Exceptions that are not renewed before expiration lapse automatically. 3.4. Recordkeeping, Retention, and Reporting All Exception records are stored in the SOR, which serves as the authoritative repository for Exception Documentation and maintains a complete audit trail of all actions and decisions. The SOR provides visibility into active and historical Exceptions for monitoring, governance oversight, audits, and examinations. Access to Exception information is restricted to the Compliance team and authorized stakeholders. Exception records are retained in accordance with all applicable regulatory and internal retention requirements. Exception Management Process 6.3 Exception Handling A Document may allow for Exceptions. Owners and/or Delegates have authority to approve Exceptions and/or exemptions to Documents, provided that the proposed Exception or exemption does not violate applicable laws and regulations. Document Exception requests must be Documented in writing, include a reason for a request, and provide a description of the controls that the Company will implement to mitigate risks associated with the Exception. Any approved Exceptions must be Documented in writing and retained with recordkeeping requirements. Any approved Exceptions or exemptions should be submitted to the Policy Team. The Owner and/or designee will inform Compliance Leadership and applicable committees, as needed, of Exceptions. The Owner and/or designee will inform Compliance Leadership and applicable committees, as needed of Exceptions. If an Exception requires renewal, approval of an extension is required.[c] 1. Exception Request Submission** * • Exception requests submitted through LogicGate Exception workflow * • Request form captures: Policy/Procedure being excepted, specific requirement being excepted, business justification, duration of Exception, and compensating controls * • Requestor must be business owner or authorized representative * • Supporting Documentation required: business case, risk assessment, and proposed mitigation measures[d] * • System automatically assigns Exception request ID and notifies Compliance team 2. Exception Evaluation**[e] * • Compliance team conducts initial completeness review within 3 business days * • Risk assessment performed to evaluate potential compliance and business risks * • Evaluation of proposed compensating controls for adequacy * • Consultation with Policy owner, Legal, Risk, and other stakeholders as needed * • Determination if Exception violates laws or regulations (automatic denial if yes) * • Assessment of precedent and impact on overall compliance program * 3. Approval Workflow** * • Approval authority determined by risk level [f]and Policy tier * • Low risk Exceptions: Policy Owner approval * • Medium risk Exceptions: Chief Compliance Officer approval * • High risk Exceptions: CPC or Board approval * • All Exceptions require Legal and Risk concurrence * • Approval decision Documented with conditions, compensating controls, and expiration date * • Denied Exceptions include rationale and alternative solutions if available 4. Documentation Requirements** * • All Exception requests and decisions Documented in LogicGate * • Approved Exceptions include: approval date, approver, conditions, compensating controls, and expiration date * • Exceptions linked to related Policy/StandardProcedure in system * • Supporting documentation attached to exception record * • Approval evidence retained per record retention Policy * • Exception register maintained with all active and historical Exceptions 5. Storage of Exception Records** * • All Exception records stored centrally in LogicGate system * • Access restricted to Compliance team and authorized stakeholders * • Exception records retained for minimum of 7 years or per regulatory requirement * • Audit trail maintained for all Exception activities * • Regular backups of Exception data performed 6. Tracking and Reporting of Exceptions** * • Active Exceptions tracked in real-time dashboard * • Automated notifications sent 30 days before Exception expiration * • Exception requestor must renew or close Exception before expiration * • Monthly Exception metrics reported to Compliance leadership * • Quarterly Exception report to CPC including: new Exceptions, expired Exceptions, risk trends, and compensating control effectiveness * • Annual Exception analysis to identify Policy improvement opportunities * • Patterns of repeated Exceptions escalated for Policy review or process change ________________ 3. Compliance & Enforcement 1. Roles and Responsibilities: Block, Inc. Roles: * Requestor: * The employee who initiates the exception request in the Syste * Document Owner: * The Owner is an individual responsible for ensuring that a Document makes its way through the entire Document lifecycle. Responsibilities include: * Identifying the need for potential new Documents and ideating; * Creating initial version of Documents; * Identifying reviewers and approvers; * Planning and conducting awareness campaigns; * Conducting scheduled reviews; * Understanding the Company’s current state of Compliance with a * Document; * Ensuring forward-looking requirements are accounted for in roadmap * planning and can be operationalized within a reasonable timeframe; * Developing and tracking the execution of the implementation plan; and * Completing attestations to the Document’s content prior to publication. * Compliance Policy and Mandatory Training Team (“Policy Team”): * The Policy Team is responsible for establishing, managing, and maintaining the Policy Governance Standards and Compliance Document inventory. Responsibilities include:. * Providing guidance and consultation in Document creation; * Facilitating review and approval in conjunction with the Owner; * Publishing and storage; * Coordinating scheduled reviews in conjunction with the Owner; * Intake of approved Exception requests; * Reporting the status of the Policy program to Compliance Leadership; * Retaining archived Documents and approvals; * Completing a quality control review for all Documents; and * Supports the Policy communication framework. * 2. Monitoring and Reporting On an ongoing basis, the Procedure Owner and/or their designee monitors activities for compliance and objective achievement. As outlined in the Compliance Management System Policy, Compliance will provide regular updates, as applicable, to the Block, Inc. Board and executive management. Reporting may cover specific information related to: * Policies and Standards * Procedures * Audit/exam findings * Issues management * Training * Complaints 1. Training [Optional Section]: All relevant personnel will receive training at least annually covering applicable requirements and guidance to implement this Procedures effectively. All training related activities are monitored by the [Team Name]. 2. Record Retention All Documents are retained according to the longer of: * Block’s Data Classification & Handling Policy * Applicable regulatory requirements 1. Exceptions [Optional Section / Sample Text]: The Procedure Owner or designee will have authority to approve Exceptions and/or exemptions to this Procedure, provided that the proposed Exception or exemption does not violate applicable laws and regulations. Exception requests must be Documented in writing, include a reason for a request, and provide a description of the controls that the Company will implement to mitigate risks associated with the Exception. The Procedure Owner is responsible for retaining evidence of Exception requests and related approvals. 2. Confidentiality [Optional Section] This Document is confidential and should only be accessed by employees. ________________ 4. Questions & Contact Information Questions: For questions or clarifications about this Procedure, please contact the Procedure Owner. Review related Documents: * Document Name * Document Name * Document Name ________________ 5. Document Details & Revisions Review and Approval: This Document shall be reviewed and approved at least triennially and on an ad hoc basis in accordance with the Compliance Document Approval Matrix. Approval History: Approver Title Type Description Date [Type @ followed by the person’s name] Select [Month date, YYYY] [Type @ followed by the person’s name] Select [Month date, YYYY] Document Details: Business Block Scope [Global/Regional and/or Entity/Region] Compliance Domain Compliance Governance Document Owner Munnu Tomy Kallany Approved By Stevi Winer [a]LG question - Should this information be entered by CT in the subsequent CT review step , given that requestor may not be aware of the exception owner criteria once defined? [b]discuss with @steviwiner@block.xyz _Assigned to steviwiner@block.xyz_ [c]current POP language for reference [d]document not requested but can be added in the system [e]to be discussed with Stevi since this is process needs to be established [f]align on criteria for risk level determination

DTP-001.title: Desktop Procedure: Document Update and Approval Workflow
DTP-001.description: Step-by-step desktop procedure for updating a document and routing it through the approval workflow in LogicGate.
DTP-001.text: ﻿Desktop Procedure: Document Update and Approval Workflow ________________ Purpose This desktop procedure outlines each stage of the document update workflow and describes the review and approval process managed by the Central Team. Workflow Map Please see Appendix for User Roles and Responsibilities in LogicGate. ________________ I. Document Drafting (Submitter Stage) Objective: To initiate the document update process by completing the draft and submitting it for quality control (QC) review. 1. The Document Submitter reviews the content and ensures all mandatory fields and data are completed in the workflow form. 2. The Submitter can select the change type as Initial Review (for new drafts), Scheduled Review (for annual refreshes), or Material/Immaterial Change (for off-cycle reviews). The document update workflow remains consistent across all change types. The only exception applies to off-cycle changes to Compliance Policy Committee (CPC) approved documents, which may be approved by the Document Owner and subsequently reported to the CPC during the next review cycle. 3. Once the Submitter initiates the document update request, the system routes the document automatically to the QC / Assign Reviewers step. ________________ II. QC / Assign Reviewers Step Objective: To assign reviewers, define approval routing, and perform governance quality check. 1. The Central Team will receive an email notification indicating that a new document is ready for review. * Access the document using the link provided in the email or from the “Drafts Under Central Team Review” section on the dashboard. 2. Review the information submitted. In the Approval Routing Level field, select all applicable approval levels from the options provided: * Document Owner * Leadership * Committee * Board * Note: the document will automatically be routed to document owner review regardless of the selection above, as this is the default mandatory path. * This selection applies only to the current draft, not the parent document. 3. If Leadership is selected as approval routing level, select the name of approver from the drop down list. 4. If Committee and Board approvals are selected, choose the specific Committees and Boards from the dropdown menus. 5. [Optional] Assign up to three stakeholder reviewers: * Include only those whose formal review must be tracked in the system. * Other informal reviewers should provide feedback directly in the shared Google Doc. * Reviewer tasks will not be created, and reviewers will not be notified until the Central Team completes the QC / Assign Reviewers step and clicks submit. * Note: The Scheduled Review Due Date on this form refers to the document itself, not the stakeholder review deadline. Reviewers will receive automated weekly reminders if their review remains incomplete for more than one week after assignment. 6. If no stakeholder reviews are required: * The Central Team must complete the governance QC at this step before submission. * Once complete, the document proceeds directly to the Approvals phase. 7. Click Proceed to Approvals to advance, or Return to Submitter if revisions are needed. * If returning to the Submitter, provide feedback or justification in the pop-up comments box. 8. Enter the Review Date and click Submit. Bypassing or Re-Initiating Stakeholder Reviews When a document request is returned to the Submitter or to the Central Team directly from any of the following steps -Document Owner Review, Leadership Approval, Committee Approval, or Board Approval - an additional option to bypass Stakeholder Review (If Applicable) becomes available the second time the record reaches the QC/Assign Reviewers step.[a][b] 1. Select “Yes” to skip the Stakeholder Review + QC step and proceed directly to the next approval stage. 2. Select “No” to continue to the Stakeholder Review + QC step. * To proceed without initiating new stakeholder reviews, leave the box labeled “Check the box if Stakeholder Reviewer #1 Has Been Updated” unchecked. Use this option when stakeholder review was already completed in the previous cycle and no further input is required. * To initiate a new stakeholder review, select “No” and enter the new stakeholder names in the provided fields, and check the box labeled “Check the box if Stakeholder Reviewer #1 Has Been Updated.” Reviewers can also be added in the next step, if needed. ________________ III. Stakeholder Review + QC Step Objective: To facilitate stakeholder feedback and complete quality checks before approval. 1. This step is only activated if stakeholder reviewers were assigned in the previous phase. Once reviewers complete their tasks, their responses populate in the stakeholder review table. * The Submit button remains inactive until all reviews are finalized. (See Managing Stakeholder Reviews DP for instructions on removing and adding new stakeholders) * If additional stakeholder reviews are created using the “Create New Stakeholder” button directly in the Stakeholder review workflow, ensure the dropdown menu above is also updated to include the name of Stakeholder #2 and #3 in the Document Update workflow. This step helps maintain accurate and complete records. * Stakeholder review progress can also be tracked in the Stakeholder Reviews – In Progress section on the dashboard. 2. When all reviews are complete: * Click Proceed to Approvals to continue. * Or, if additional revisions are needed, select Return to Submitter. ________________ IV. Document Owner and Leadership Approver Reviews From QC/ Assign Reviewers and/or Stakeholder Review + QC Steps, the Document Owner (when the Submitter is the same individual as the Document Owner, the Document Owner review is bypassed) and Leadership review requests are routed to their assigned users. Approvals occur sequentially, and the document can be returned to the Central Team or the Submitter at any stage. From the Leadership Review step, it can also be returned to the Document Owner if further updates are required. Note: Following Document Owner/ Leadership Approval, if Committee (excluding CPC) or Board review is required for a document, a PDF of the redline is provided to the Submitter/Document Owner to share with the relevant Committee/Board for approval. ________________ V. Committee Review Step (if applicable) Objective: To capture the committee’s decision, review date, and supporting evidence. 1. The Central Team will be prompted to enter the Committee review decision and review date if “Committee” was selected under Approval Routing Levels field in the QC/ Assign Reviewers step. If multiple committees reviewed the document, record the most recent review date. 2. Once Approval is selected, enter the approval dates by Committee/s identified earlier in the workflow and upload the corresponding evidence of approval. Click Submit to move to the next step. ________________ VI. Board Review Step (if applicable) Objective: To document the Board’s review and approval decision. 1. The workflow will proceed to the Board Review step, if ”Board” was selected under Approval Routing Levels field in the QC/ Assign Reviewers step.The Board Review step functions similarly to the Committee Review. The Central Team will be prompted to enter the review decision and date then input the relevant Board meeting date/s and upload supporting evidence to proceed to the next step. * Note: For Board-approved documents, a clean PDF of the updated version is created and stored in the Published PDF folder, and a downloaded copy is provided to the Document Submitter/Owner to share with the Board for review and approval (this is best practise since GDrive links may have access limitations when shared externally). The document should not be made viewable within Block until the publication step. Before generating the PDF: * Save a copy of the master Google Doc (with comments/redlines) in the Archived Google Doc folder. * Accept all suggestions to create a clean version. * Generate the final PDF from this clean version and store it in the appropriate Central Drive folder. ________________ VII. Publication Step Objective: To finalize the approved document for publication and archive prior versions. 1. Select a publication decision from the following options: * Approve for Publication * Return to Central Team (returns QC or Assign Reviewers) * Return to Committee Review * Return to Board Review 2. When Approve for Publication is selected, a new field appears prompting entry of the final approval date. Refer to the approval table above and enter the date that corresponds to the document’s most recent approval. 3. Enter the publication date and provide the link to the published PDF. 4. Review the publication checklist 5. Click Submit to: * Move the document into the Active Version Repository. * Automatically retire the previous version. * Create a new In Progress record for the next review cycle. ________________ VIII. Input Published Links Objective: To update the parent workflow with the published document link. 1. The Central Team will receive an email notification prompting them to input the published PDF link. * Alternatively, the Central Team can access the task from their Dashboard. 2. Copy the link to the published PDF with draft status = active, and paste it into the Published Document field. * This step must be done manually because the system cannot automatically transfer data between workflows.[c] 3. Click Submit to update the published PDF links. ________________ IX. Drafts Ready for Assignment Objective: Assign new draft for the next review cycle. 1. A new draft record is created automatically when the publication step is completed. 2. The Central Team will receive an email notification and can also access the new draft via their Dashboard. 3. Enter the Scheduled Review Date for the new draft. Refer to the document history on this step to determine the correct date according to the guidance provided in the form. For initial draft assignment, enter the target approval date for the document. * This date serves as the basis for all notifications and reporting related to the upcoming review cycle. Ensure that the information entered is accurate and complete. 4. Enter the Submitter and Document Owner information. Verify that these assignment details are correct for the next review cycle before submitting. 5. When Submit is selected, the new draft is assigned to the Submitter and appears under the My Work, or alternatively in the “My Policies”, “My Standards”, or “My Procedures” section of the default All Employees dashboard ________________ X. Reporting To view details about document drafts: 1. Go to the Reports section in LogicGate. 2. Select Table Report. 3. Search for the following table reports to view all drafts by document type: * All Policy Drafts * All Standard Drafts * All Procedure Draft * Each document will have multiple line items, depending on the number of drafts that have been created and/or completed in the system. 4. Use the Draft Status field to determine the records current state: * Active – Current approved version * Retired – Prior approved version * In Progress – Draft assigned to the Submitter or Owner for updates ________________ Document Details: Business Block Scope Global Domain Governance Document Owner Munnu Tomy Kallany Approved By Stevi Winer ________________ Appendix: Roles and Responsibilities Employee: All full-time employees are provisioned system access. Employees may view documents and initiate requests for documents creation, retirements, and exceptions in the system. Submitter : Acts as the Document Owner’s delegate and initiates requests for new Policies, Standards, or Procedures, as well as revisions to existing documents. Provides initial content, rationale for creation, or description of changes, and incorporates feedback from stakeholders and approvers. Documents are assigned to the Submitter by default and appear in their queue for updates. In some cases, the Submitter and Document Owner may be the same individual. Document Owner: Responsible for attesting to the accuracy and completeness of the document content and ensuring the document is reviewed and refreshed according to the established schedule. Central Team: Serves as the governance team overseeing document reviews, quality, and compliance. Documents may fall under different Central Teams depending on sub-type, domain, or other attributes. Stakeholder Reviewer: Provides subject-matter input, review, and sign-off for the document within their area of expertise. Stakeholder Reviewers are typically selected by the Document Owner or Submitter. Leadership Approver: Reviews the document, provides feedback, and grants the necessary approval Committee: Acts as the governing committee with oversight responsibility and approval authority for applicable documents. For Board approved documents, the Committee may review, provide feedback, and recommend for approval by the Board. Board: The governing board(s) responsible for final review and approval of applicable documents. [a]Should we explain why you would want to do this just for context? [b]updated [c]@pbuchmann@block.xyz _Assigned to pbuchmann@block.xyz_

DTP-002.title: Desktop Procedure: Extension Workflow
DTP-002.description: Step-by-step desktop procedure for submitting a due date extension request and the Central Team review and approval workflow in LogicGate.
DTP-002.text: ﻿Desktop Procedure: Extension Workflow ________________ Purpose This procedure outlines the process for submitting an extension request for a document and describes the subsequent review and approval workflow managed by the Central Team. Workflow Map ________________ I. Initiate Extension Request from Document Drafting Step 1. Log in to LogicGate 2. Select the relevant document that requires an extension * Only users who are part of the workflow can request an extension. The document is typically assigned to the Submitter and/or Document Owner. 3. Open the document record 4. Locate the “Create New Extension” button at the bottom of the step to begin the process ________________ II. Request Extension On[a][b] the Extension Request page, fill in all required fields: 1. Requested Extension Date – Specify the new desired review date 2. Reason for Extension – Provide a clear explanation for requesting the extension 3. Document Owner Confirmation – Indicate whether you are the Document Owner: * Select Yes if you are the Document Owner submitting this request * Select No if you are not the Document Owner. If No is selected, the request will automatically route to the Document Owner for review before being processed by the Central Team. 4. Requestor Attestation – Sign and click Submit to send the request for review ________________ III. Document Owner Review 1. When a request is received, the Document Owner has the following options: * Return to Submitter – Send back to the requester for corrections or clarifications * When selecting Return at any step, the user is always required to provide justification. * Reject Request – Decline the extension request * Send for Approvals – Forward the request for Central Team review and processing ________________ IV. Central Team Review 1. During this stage, the Central Team will assess the request’s rationale and take one of the following actions: * Send to Compliance Leadership for Approval * Return to Submitter * Return to Document Owner * Reject Request 2. If the Central Team selects Send to Compliance Leadership for Approval, they must: * Enter the Review Date and Leadership Approver information. * Update the Requested Extension Date to reflect a maximum of 6 months from the document’s scheduled review date (as only one 6-month extension may be granted for a document during review cycle).[c] [Refer to the Document Details section at the top of the form to confirm the scheduled review date.] ________________ V. Leadership Approval If the Central Team routes the request for Leadership review: 1. The Leadership Approver is automatically notified once the Central Team completes its initial review. 2. The Leadership Reviewer can then: * Approve the extension request * Reject the request * Return to Central Team for further review * Once routed to Leadership, no further action is required from the Central Team. * If the Leadership Reviewer approves the request, it is automatically routed to the Extension Repository for recordkeeping. ________________ VI. Reporting To view details about approved exceptions: 1. Go to the Reports section in LogicGate. 2. Select Table Report. 3. Search for Active Exceptions table reports to access the exceptions repository. This is the central location for tracking all approved exception requests and serves as a reference for audits or future reviews. ________________ Document Details: Business Block Scope Global Domain Governance Document Owner Munnu Tomy Kallany Approved By Stevi Winer ________________ Appendix: Roles and Responsibilities Employee: All full-time employees are provisioned system access. Employees may view documents and initiate requests for documents creation, retirements, and exceptions in the system. Submitter : Acts as the Document Owner’s delegate and initiates requests for new Policies, Standards, or Procedures, as well as revisions to existing documents. Provides initial content, rationale for creation, or description of changes, and incorporates feedback from stakeholders and approvers. Documents are assigned to the Submitter by default and appear in their queue for updates. In some cases, the Submitter and Document Owner may be the same individual. Document Owner: Responsible for attesting to the accuracy and completeness of the document content and ensuring the document is reviewed and refreshed according to the established schedule. Central Team: Serves as the governance team overseeing document reviews, quality, and compliance. Documents may fall under different Central Teams depending on sub-type, domain, or other attributes. Stakeholder Reviewer: Provides subject-matter input, review, and sign-off for the document within their area of expertise.
